en: let's talk about common English mistakes which you are making and that is stopping
you from speaking professionally number one it's never reply back it's always
reply or reward without the back number two it is never more better better is
already in the comparative degree so you just say better you cannot use two
comparative words refrain from saying it was a blunder mistake because blunder is
already a mistake so it's a blunder or it's a mistake be careful while using
fewer and less fewer is used for items which can be counted and less is used
for items which cannot be counted so fewer people less milk if you say Shreya
and me are going to the gym or me and Shreya are going to the gym then that's
wrong so Shreya and I are going to the gym the name of the person will come
first and then I that's correct English take care of these errors and speak
